2605.06426.title: From 124 Million Tokens to 1,021 Neologisms: A Large-Scale Pipeline for Automatic Neologism Detection
2605.06426.paper_summary: We present a scalable, modular pipeline for automatic neologism detection that combines rule-based filtering with LLM classification. The pipeline is grounded in two complementary word-formation frameworks, grammatical and extra-grammatical morphology, which jointly define the scope of what counts as a neologism and inform a four-class classification scheme (neologism, entity, foreign, none). While designed to be modular and transferable at the architectural level, the pipeline is instantiated on 527 million English-language Reddit posts spanning 2005-2024. From this corpus, we extract 124.6 million unique tokens and reduce them by over 99.99% to yield 1,021 neologism candidates, a set small enough for manual expert verification. Multiple LLMs independently classify each candidate via majority vote, with a final verification step, revealing substantial cross-model disagreement and highlighting the challenge of operationalizing neologism detection at scale. Manual annotation of all 1,021 candidates confirms that 599 (58.7%) are genuine lexical innovations. The pipeline code, vocabulary compilation scripts, and the annotated candidate list are available at https://github.com/DiegoRossini/neologism-pipeline.
2605.06426.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了一個可擴展的模組化流程，用於自動新詞檢測，結合了基於規則的過濾和LLM分類。該流程基於兩個互補的詞形成框架，即語法和超語法形態學，這共同定義了什麼算作新詞的範疇，並提供了一個四類分類方案（新詞、實體、外來詞、無）。雖然設計上旨在於架構層面上模組化和可轉移，但該流程是在2005年至2024年之間的5.27億條英語Reddit帖子上實現的。從這個語料庫中，我們提取了1.246億個獨特標記，並將其減少超過99.99%，產生1,021個新詞候選項，這個集合足夠小以便進行人工專家驗證。多個LLM獨立地通過多數投票對每個候選項進行分類，並進行最終驗證步驟，顯示出模型之間存在實質性的分歧，突顯了在大規模操作中實現新詞檢測的挑戰。對所有1,021個候選項的人工標註確認有599個（58.7%）是真正的詞彙創新。該流程的代碼、詞彙編譯腳本和標註候選項列表可在https://github.com/DiegoRossini/neologism-pipeline獲得。
2605.06426.author: Diego Rossini et.al.
2605.06426.authors: Diego Rossini, Lonneke van der Plas
